subject: reponse to Wlodek
body:
FYI

---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Ben Goertzel <ben@goertzel.org>
Date: Thu, Apr 17, 2008 at 12:49 PM
Subject: Re: Quirky question from a wealthy AI patron, Hugo, "China card"
To: Wlodzislaw Duch <wduch@is.umk.pl>
Cc: Alexei V Samsonovich <asamsono@gmu.edu>, Hugo deGaris
<profhugodegaris@yahoo.com>, Moshe Looks <moshe@metacog.org>, Pei Wang
<dr.peiwang@gmail.com>, Georges Harik <georges@gmail.com>, Martin
Pelikan <pelikan@cs.umsl.edu>, Eric Baum <ebaum@fastmail.fm>, William
Schuler <schuler@cs.umn.edu>, dkroy@media.mit.edu, Wlodek Duch
<wduch@phys.uni.torun.pl>, laird@umich.edu, "Sam S. Adams"
<ssadams@us.ibm.com>, Selmer Bringsjord <brings@rpi.edu>,
samsonovich@cox.net


Hi guys,

 I agree that a focused project is needed.

 However, an obvious problem is that many of us have our own quite
 specific ideas about what the focus of the focused project should be
 ;-)

 Thus it seems that, if an AI Manhattan Project were to be funded, one
 would want to divide up the work into at least three phases

 Phase 1)
 Here we just need researchers not software engineers -- although
 participation of 1 or 2 folks with software architecture and computer
 systems background would be very valuable, to keep planning grounded
 in reality.

 The goal here would be to arrive at the commonly accepted AI architecture.

 At the end of this phase, quite possibly, some of the original
 research team might leave the project due to finding the architecture
 decided-upon disagrees too profoundly with their own ideas.  I
 wouldn't consider this ideal but wouldn't be shocked if it happened,
 nor consider it an indicator of failure.  I think there are many
 possible approaches to AGI, and concrete choices need to be made based
 on our limited current understanding.

 Phase 2)
 High-level software systems design

 Phase 3)
 Begin a long process of integrated, dynamically interactive software
 implementation, testing, teaching, theorizing and designing....


 I'm not suggesting a "waterfall" software process where things are
 designed first and then implemented.  Obviously this can't work for
 AGI.  But I'm suggesting that given the diversity of opinions and
 approaches among AGI researchers, an initial phase of coordination and
 collaboration to decide on an overall coherent design accepted by a
 group of researchers, would be a good idea.

 -- Ben





 On Thu, Apr 17, 2008 at 9:57 AM, Wlodzislaw Duch <wduch@is.umk.pl> wrote:
 >
 >
 >
 >
 > Guys,
 >
 >
 >
 > I quite agree with Alexei that we need a focused project, and wrote it
 > already directly to Ben.
 >
 >
 >
 > I'd suggest Web page references to important projects and some info about 5
 > people you're recommending, perhaps some justification why they would make a
 > useful team.
 >
 >
 >
 > Wlodek
 >
 >

On Thu, Apr 17, 2008 at 9:57 AM, Wlodzislaw Duch wduch@is.umk.pl wrote:

Guys,

I quite agree with Alexei that we need a focused project, and wrote it
already directly to Ben.

I'd suggest Web page references to important projects and some info about 5
people you're recommending, perhaps some justification why they would make a
useful team.

Wlodek
